Meet Yosr, a 3rd-year Business Administration student at the UvA. When she started her studies in 2022, she chose not to move to Amsterdam. Instead, she commutes every day from her home in a town nearby Amsterdam to the campus by train and other public transport.

Met zijn project Shared Beginnings onderzoekt econoom Zichen Deng hoe recent uitgebreid vaderschapsverlof in Nederland uitpakt in de praktijk. 

“We willen inzicht krijgen in de bredere maatschappelijke effecten van dit beleid. Niet alleen op korte termijn, maar ook op de lange termijn.”

Met Shared Beginnings wil Deng bijdragen aan een meer inclusief en onderbouwd gezinsbeleid. “Beleid gebaseerd op data, niet op aannames. Dat kan helpen om de balans tussen werk en gezin eerlijker te maken, voor vaders én moeders. En uiteindelijk: voor gezondere kinderen en een sterke samenleving.”

Meer info over Deng’s onderzoek via de link in bio 🔗

🌟Kaya Han Taş is a masterstudent who discovered a planet during his master thesis project at the University of Amsterdam. Using the radial velocity method, the transit method and imaging with several instruments around the globe, he detected TOI 2431 b.
Read the full story on the NOVA website (link in bio). 

Research: Tas et al. (2025)
Image: NOVA/ Paul Baecke 
Video: Paul Baecke voor NOVA

Celebrate Liberation Day with us at the Allard Pierson 🕊️
 
Join us for a freedom lunch centred around connection, storytelling, and creativity. Pick up a can of freedom soup to take home, or take a seat at the table to share stories, listen to poetry, and take part in an embroidery workshop.
 
Featuring a poetry reading by Bernice Vreedzaam – who will write a poem especially for this occasion, – a lunch talk on Surinamica, and a collective embroidery workshop by Suelae Robinson, where we will create a patchwork reflecting on freedom and resistance.
 
🕊  Programme
11.00 – 16.00: Freedom soup pick-up point and space to gather and share a meal
12.00 – 12.15: Poetry reading by Bernice Vreedzaam
13.00 – 13.30: Lunch talk by Isabelle Best, curator of the Surinamica collection
14.00 – 15.00: Stitching Freedom: A Collective Patchwork Workshop Inspired by Maroon Resistance, by Suelae Robinson
 
📅 Tuesday, 5 May 2026
🕒 11.00 – 16.00
📍 Allard Pierson (Oude Turfmarkt 129)
🎟️ Free, walk-in

By taking part in the Student Well-being survey, you can share what is going well and what our uni can improve, so that everyone feels at home at the UvA. 

You can also win a prize by taking part in the Survey: 
⭐️ 1x Museum Pass (valid for one year)
⭐️ 1x ARTIS membership (valid for one year).
⭐️ 2x Cineville pass (valid for six months). The best part about the Cineville pass is that you can share it -you’ll win a pass for yourself and you may give one away to a fellow student.

➡️ How to find the Student Well-being survey? You’ll have received an email from Qualtrics, the UvA’s survey tool, on 14 April. Completing the survey takes 10 minutes and you can do so until 26 April.

🐦🎵 The birds behind the sounds of spring

Spring has started and the birds are singing! But, which birds do we hear, exactly? And why do they start singing in the first place?

According to bird expert Joris Buis the common chiff-chaff is one of the first birds you can hear, starting in February and recognizable by its song: “chiff-chaff chiff-chaff” 🎶

Another one: the great tit (koolmees). And his song sounds like ‘a tiny pump’: two high notes that rhythmically go up and down, ‘tee-too tee-too’.

His? Yes, in the Netherlands and in Europe it’s mainly male birds who sing. Their goal:
🔹 Attracting females
🔹 Scaring away other males

Fun fact: singing is so important to male songbirds, that their brains (mainly the part responsible for their vocals) and even their balls significantly grow. The latter can even become 10 times its original size 🤯

Which birds have you spotted already the last few days?

#springtime #birds #amsterdam

We’re reposting this one because tomorrow it’s time to vote 🗳️ and we want you to walk into that voting booth feeling confident.

Thinking of asking ChatGPT who to vote for? We’d recommend watching this video first 👀 Spoiler: we’re not big fans of that idea 🤖❌

Instead, use one of these reliable voting guides:

🔵 StemWijzer (VoteMatch) by ProDemos, the institute for democracy and the rule of law 
🧭 Kieskompas (Election Compass) an independent research institute with academic roots 
📰 Stemchecker by de Volkskrant 
✅ Check je stem (Check Your Vote) based on previous motions in the Dutch parliament 
📋 Kieswijzer, produced by the parliamentary editorial teams of AD and Nu.nl (DPG Media)

And don’t forget to vote in the local council elections tomorrow, Wednesday, 18 March! Not just people with Dutch passports can vote in the municipal elections so check if you’re eligible online or check our video from a few days ago.

Would you use AI to help you decide who to vote for? 🤖

In this video, political scientist Zahra Runderkamp explains the reasons why women are underrepresented in local politics and why this is a problem. 

Zahra is the leading expert in the field of representation of women and ethnic minorities in politics and their representation in the media in the Netherlands. She defended her thesis titled ‘Personal institutions, political lives. Diversity without inclusion in Dutch politics’ at our university last summer. Yesterday, she won the Dutch Woman in Media award 2025. 

Tomorrow, the city council elections take place and you can let your voice be heard at polling stations all around the city (also on our campuses). You can vote for the city council if all of these apply to you:

🗳️ You are 18 years or older.
🗳️ You are registered as a resident of Amsterdam.
🗳️ You are an EU citizen, or you have been registered in the Netherlands without interruption for at least 5 years.

Another way to find out if you’re eligible to vote, is to check whether you’ve received a voting pass in your mail. 🗳️

Meet future UvA students! 🫶 During the Open Campus Day last Friday, we asked them to make a Polaroid and share their thoughts about studying in Amsterdam, uni life and the study programmes they’re interested in. Thanks everyone and hopefully we’ll see you next year! 🙌
Say hi to all (possible) future UvA students! 🙌🎓

You can vote for the city council if all of these apply to you:

🗳️ You are 18 years or older.
🗳️ You are registered as a resident of Amsterdam.
🗳️ You are an EU citizen, or you have been registered in the Netherlands without interruption for at least 5 years.

Another way to find out if you’re eligible to vote, is to check whether you’ve received a voting pass in your mail. 

We encourage you to go vote, because you can let your voice be heard regarding local policies for the next four years. Think about housing policies, urban planning and design, waste management, and so much more. You can check which party represents your views by, for example, filling in the questionnaire of amsterdam.kieskompas.nl or another (trustworthy) voting tool of your municipality (check your municipality website for more advice on this). Be cautious when using AI tools for voting advise because research shows that these can contain incorrect information regarding political parties and their standpoints. 

The turnout in local elections is often low (in 2022 it was around 50%), especially among young people. So let’s make a difference, use our right to vote and let our voices be heard coming 18 March.

The polling stations in which you can cast your vote will be all around Amsterdam. It’s also possible to vote in our University Library in the University Quarter @uva_library, @creaamsterdam at REC and at Science Park. You can find all the stations in Amsterdam via stembureaus.amsterdam.nl.

De Nieuwe Mensa celebrates its first anniversary! What started as a critical student initiative, offering an affordable alternative for food and drink on campus, has grown into De Nieuwe Mensa: a popular and vibrant spot on the Roeterseiland Campus. For a full year, students have been able to enjoy affordable, vegan meals, made by students. A milestone worth celebrating 🎉

We visited Leo, one of the chefs at De Nieuwe Mensa, and asked him to share one of the Nieuwe Mensa’s recipes so that we can try to make this ourselves at home. It’s a perfect meal to make during this ‘Wissel ’ns Wat’ week (previously ‘de week zonder vlees’ or ‘the week without meat), a national campaign that aims to encourage us to make vegan meals a more consistent part of our diets. 🌱

Full recipe in the comments! 🫶

Everyone at our uni deserves to feel safe, respected, and heard. Not feeling that way right now? You’re not alone. A confidential advisor is here to support you and help you take the next step.

A confidential advisor can: • listen to your story and feelings • help stop unwanted behaviour before it grows
• can explain the different options for a solution to you • act only with your clear permission • connect you to professional support • guide you if you want to file a formal complaint

Everything you share stays strictly confidential. You can find more info about our confidential advisers via the link in bio.

In deze aflevering van Net Echt duiken we in The Godfather. Niet zomaar een film, maar dé blauwdruk voor hoe we naar de maffia kijken. De film beïnvloedde niet alleen het publiek, maar ook de echte onderwereld. Criminelen gingen zich kleden als Don Corleone en toen de criminelen in de Hollywood films de wapens ineens schuin gingen vasthouden, gingen de echte criminelen dat ook opeens doen. Samen met UvA-hoogleraar strafrecht Sven Brinkhoff en misdaadjournalist bij NRC Jan Meeus onderzoeken we:

🔹Hoe realistisch is het beeld van de maffia in The Godfather?
🔹Zien we dat soort structuren ook in de Nederlandse onderwereld?
🔹Hoe beïnvloedt fictie de echte misdaad en de opsporing?
🔹En: wat doet onze fascinatie voor deze wereld met het vertrouwen in justitie en journalistiek?

De werkelijkheid en fictie spiegelen elkaar. Beïnvloeden elkaar. Maar hoe ver gaat dat? 

Luister de hele aflevering via de link in bio.

🎂 Today we’re celebrating UvA’s 394th birthday! 

It all started in 1632 with the founding of the Athenaeum Illustre by Gerardus Vossius and Caspar Barlaeus in the Agnietenkapel. Today, almost four centuries later, we’re the largest university in the Netherlands with 41,000 students and thousands of staff. All grown up! 

We’re celebrating the occasion, the Dies Natalis, with a celebration featuring speeches and rituals like wearing academic robes and awarding honorary doctorates to exceptional people. 

In 2032 we’ll be celebrating our 400th birthday! 🎉 

🤖 AI helped us create parts of this content, in line with UvA’s guidelines for responsible AI use. 

📍 Follow our Stories today for live coverage of Dies 🔴 Or watch via livestream (link in bio) 

🎥 @christiaankanis 

#UvA #DiesNatalis #394years #UvA394 #UniversityOfAmsterdam #Amsterdam #Birthday

Tomorrow, the University of Amsterdam celebrates its 394th Dies Natalis 🎓 and we are incredibly proud to award an honorary doctorate to Corinne Vigreux. As co-founder of TomTom, Corinne fundamentally transformed how we navigate and helped millions worldwide find their way. 

But her impact extends far beyond technology. Through the Sofronie Foundation, she champions social mobility, and by founding Codam, an innovative tuition-free coding school with no admission requirements, she democratises access to tech education. This honorary doctorate recognises her pioneering role in technology and her tireless commitment to building a more inclusive society. 🌟 

🎥 @christiaankanis

Meet Bas, a Local Buddy at @esnamsterdam and a student of Communication Science and Applied Psychology. Bas has been a Local Buddy for 2,5 years now. To him, the Buddy Program is about making friends, sharing the city, discovering cultures together, and creating genuine connections.

“I made so many great memories during my student years that I wish I’d had from the start, so I wanted to help exchange and new international students make the same memories when they come to Amsterdam. So I became a Local Buddy.” 

Do you also want to become Local Buddy and help new international students feel at home in Amsterdam? Join the ESN Amsterdam Buddy Program!

As a buddy, you’ll support new international and exchange students as they settle into life in Amsterdam. Whether you’re Dutch or an international yourself who doesn’t speak Dutch – everyone who loves helping others is welcome to become a Local Buddy! The most important thing is being open to new cultures and ready to make someone feel at home. As a buddy, you’ll make new friends, learn from each other, and create unforgettable memories!

📱How to join?

Download the ESN Papaya app from the App Store or Google Play Store, create an account, add your phone number, and select the Buddy Program in the menu. Need help? Check out the Buddy Program section on our website 🌐 https://esn-amsterdam.nl/programs/buddy-program/
